---
title: "Two paths to Python dashboards:"
subtitle: <a href="https://github.com/posit-dev/py-shiny/"><img src="img/logo_shiny.svg.png" width="50"></a> &nbsp;versus &nbsp;<a href="https://github.com/plotly/dash"><img src="img/logo_dash.png" width="170"></a>
author:
  - Marie-Hélène Burle
  - Alex Razoumov
---

:::{.def}

Interactive dashboards are a powerful way to explore data, communicate results, and build web applications directly from Python. Two popular frameworks for this purpose are [Python Shiny](https://github.com/posit-dev/py-shiny/) and [Plotly Dash](https://github.com/plotly/dash), each offering different approaches to building reactive interfaces and interactive visualizations.

In this webinar, we will compare these two frameworks through a small set of practical examples. Rather than providing a comprehensive feature-by-feature review, we will implement the same simple dashboards in both frameworks and examine how their design philosophies translate into code structure and reactivity models, and how similar functionality can be implemented in each framework.

:::

[Plotly Dash webpage by Alex](https://folio.vastcloud.org/plotly1/plotly-04-dash.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

[Python Shiny slides by Marie](wb_shiny_slides.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary} [(This presentation may take a little time to load.)]{.inlinenote}
<br><br>

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/embed/exp1b6HQjlI >}}

<br>
*[Slides content](wb_shiny_content.qmd) for easier browsing.*

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "LearningResource",
  "name": "Two paths to Python dashboards: Shiny vs Dash",
  "description": "Interactive dashboards are a powerful way to explore data, communicate results, and build web applications directly from Python. Two popular frameworks for this purpose are Python Shiny and Plotly Dash, each offering different approaches to building reactive interfaces and interactive visualizations.\n\nIn this webinar, we will compare these two frameworks through a small set of practical examples. Rather than providing a comprehensive feature-by-feature review, we will implement the same simple dashboards in both frameworks and examine how their design philosophies translate into code structure and reactivity models, and how similar functionality can be implemented in each framework.",
  "url": "https://mint.westdri.ca/python/wb_shiny",
  "learningResourceType": [
    "slides",
	"video"
  ],
  "inLanguage": "en",
  "license": "https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/",
  "provider": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "SFU Research Computing Group"
  },
  "keywords": "Python, Shiny, Dash, dashboard, app",
  "author": [
    {
      "@type": "Person",
      "name": "Marie-Hélène Burle"
    },
    {
      "@type": "Person",
      "name": "Alex Razoumov"
    }
  ],
  "educationalLevel": "advanced"
}
</script>
